title: Add minZoom={0.1} to ReactFlow to enable dot LOD view
description: BaseNode.tsx has a dot LOD tier at zoom < 0.3, but ReactFlow's default minZoom is 0.5, making the dot view unreachable. Add minZoom={0.1} prop to the ReactFlow component in DesignCanvas.tsx. This enables the 3-tier LOD system (full > 0.6, simplified 0.3-0.6, dot < 0.3) to work as designed.
acceptanceCriteria: User can zoom out to 10% zoom level
Dot LOD (3x3px colored circles) renders at zoom < 0.3
Simplified LOD renders at zoom 0.3-0.6
Full detail renders at zoom > 0.6
Performance remains smooth at all zoom levels with 50+ nodes

title: Add focus+context hover — dim non-connected nodes when hovering a node
description: Inspired by D3 force-directed graph pattern and Netflix Vizceral. When hovering a node, dim all NON-CONNECTED nodes to 20% opacity and highlight connected edges at full saturation. This reveals topology at a glance without clicking. Implement via CSS opacity transitions on React Flow node wrappers. Use canvas-store to determine connected nodes for the hovered node.
acceptanceCriteria: Hovering a node dims all non-connected nodes to 20% opacity
Connected nodes remain at 100% opacity
Connected edges glow with category color
Non-connected edges dim to 20% opacity
Transition is smooth (200ms ease-out)
Mouse-leave restores all nodes to full opacity

title: Add spring-physics button feedback to CanvasToolbar
description: Toolbar buttons use simple CSS transitions (150ms all) with no physics-based feedback. Inspired by Linear App and Apple iOS 26 motion design. Add: (1) Scale-down to 0.95 on press (snappy spring), (2) Scale-back to 1.0 on release (with slight overshoot from bouncy spring), (3) Subtle glow on active state using box-shadow with category color. Use existing motion.ts springs: snappy for press, bouncy for release.
acceptanceCriteria: All toolbar buttons have spring-based press feedback (scale 0.95 → 1.0)
Active button has subtle glow effect
Animation uses motion.ts spring configs (snappy for press, bouncy for release)
No jank during rapid button clicking
Respects prefers-reduced-motion (instant transitions)

title: Add entrance animation to EmptyState with gentle scale-in and fade
description: EmptyState.tsx has zero animation (score 1/3 on VA2). Add a gentle entrance: (1) Icon scales from 0.8 to 1.0 with smooth spring, (2) Heading fades in from opacity 0 with 100ms delay, (3) Description fades in with 200ms delay, (4) Buttons slide up from y:8 with 300ms delay. Use motion.ts stagger pattern (panelContent: 40ms). Total entrance ~500ms. Respects prefers-reduced-motion (instant appear).
acceptanceCriteria: EmptyState has a gentle entrance animation (scale + fade + slide)
Staggered: icon first, then heading, then description, then buttons
Total animation duration ~500ms
Respects prefers-reduced-motion (instant appear)
Animation only plays once (not on re-render)

title: Add skeleton loading states to simulation panels before metrics compute
description: Inspired by Smashing Magazine real-time dashboard patterns. When simulation starts, there's a brief moment where metrics haven't computed yet. Currently shows either nothing or 0 values. Add skeleton loading states: gray animated placeholders shaped like metric pills (RPS bar, latency number, sparkline shape). Use the shimmer technique: linear-gradient animation from gray-6 to gray-8. Skeleton transitions smoothly to actual values when ready.
acceptanceCriteria: Simulation dashboard shows skeleton placeholders for first 500ms
Skeleton shape matches actual metric pill layout (width, height, spacing)
Shimmer animation on skeletons (left-to-right gradient sweep)
Smooth crossfade from skeleton to actual values (200ms)
Skeleton respects dark/light theme

title: Add shimmer highlight effect to active simulation edges
description: Inspired by Linear App's shimmer effect. During simulation, active edges (carrying traffic) should have a moving gradient highlight along the stroke path. Implement via animated stroke-dashoffset combined with a luminous gradient mask. This adds premium polish beyond simple dashed-line animation. Use CSS animation: stroke-dashoffset from 100% to 0% over 2 seconds, with a lighter stroke color in the dashgap region.
acceptanceCriteria: Active edges during simulation have a shimmer highlight moving along the path
Shimmer color is a lighter version of the edge's protocol color
Animation loop is smooth (2s duration, linear, infinite)
Does not conflict with existing edge animations (dashed pattern, hover glow)
Respects prefers-reduced-motion (static glow instead of shimmer)

title: Improve light theme to be first-class (not secondary to dark)
description: Browser testing found light theme scores 6.5/10 — functional but 'washed out' compared to the vibrant dark theme. The node color scheme loses contrast on white backgrounds. Fix: (1) Increase saturation of node category colors by 10% in light mode, (2) Add subtle colored borders to nodes (not just the category border — add a hairline inner shadow), (3) Darken edge labels for readability, (4) Add a warm off-white canvas background (#FAFAF9) instead of pure white, (5) Review all foreground-muted text for WCAG AA contrast on light background.
acceptanceCriteria: Light theme nodes have distinct, high-contrast category colors
Edge labels are readable on light background (check WCAG AA contrast)
Canvas background uses warm off-white (not pure white)
Overall light theme visual quality matches dark theme (both 7.5+/10)
All text passes WCAG AA contrast ratio (4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large)

title: Add template architecture preview thumbnails to gallery cards
description: Browser testing found template gallery cards have no visual preview of the architecture (just text title + description). Adding a small thumbnail showing the node-edge layout would: (1) Help users visually scan for familiar patterns, (2) Make the gallery more visually appealing, (3) Differentiate from competitor text-only galleries. Generate thumbnails from template JSON on build: render nodes as small colored dots at their positions, edges as thin lines. Store as inline SVG or tiny canvas render.
acceptanceCriteria: Each template card shows a small (120x80px) architecture preview
Preview shows node positions as colored dots and edges as lines
Colors match the actual category colors
Previews generated statically at build time (not runtime)
Gallery still loads fast (thumbnails are lightweight SVG/canvas)
